Good morning, It was another amazing week! Matt Belloni revealed that the forthcoming Michael Jackson biopic has some major third act problems; Eric Gardner deciphered a new twist in the Lively-Baldoni imbroglio;

Jan 25, 2025

Jon Kelly

Good morning,

It was another amazing week! Matt Belloni revealed that the forthcoming Michael Jackson biopic has some major third act problems; Eriq Gardner deciphered a new twist in the Lively - Baldoni imbroglio; Lauren Sherman captured the latest designer musical chairs intrigue; Rachel Strugatz assessed the Kardashian beauty portfolio; Sarah Shapiro tried to explain the appeal of Vuori; Marion Maneker previewed an art world boardroom drama; Julie Davich dug into Kathleen Kennedy \xe2\x80\x99s big art sale; John Ourand ran the numbers on the NFL\xe2\x80\x99s playoff ratings declines; and Dylan Byers channeled the anxiety inside a shrinking CNN. Meanwhile, John Heilemann talked Trump with Joe Scarborough ; Tara Palmeri dissected the Dems\xe2\x80\x99 Fetterman fascination; and Peter Hamby envisioned the future of American oligarchy.

Check out these stories, and others, via the links below. And stick around for the backstory on how it all came together.

FASHION

Lauren Sherman contemplates Simone Bellotti \xe2\x80\x99s legacy at Bally.

and\xe2\x80\xa6

Rachel Strugatz evaluates the Kardashian - Jenner beauty portfolio.

meanwhile\xe2\x80\xa6

Sarah Shapiro details the Vuori mega-growth story.

ART MARKET

Marion Maneker analyzes the battle for Artnet and the diversifying market for American art .

and\xe2\x80\xa6

Julie Davich picks through Kathleen Kennedy \xe2\x80\x99s art collection.

HOLLYWOOD

Matt Belloni breaks the news on a new Michael Jackson - adjacent scandal.

and\xe2\x80\xa6

Eriq Gardner chronicles the Drake \xe2\x80\x93 Kendrick Lamar legal spat.

meanwhile\xe2\x80\xa6

Scott Mendelson gathers the industry\xe2\x80\x99s biggest box office questions for \xe2\x80\x9925.

WALL STREET

Bill Cohan peruses the bank-succession league tables.

MEDIA

Dylan Byers reflects on CNN\xe2\x80\x99s year of magical thinking.

and\xe2\x80\xa6

John Ourand unpacks the NFL\xe2\x80\x99s surprising ratings dip.

WASHINGTON

Tara Palmeri scrutinizes John Fetterman \xe2\x80\x99s surprise ascent.

and\xe2\x80\xa6

Peter Hamby articulates the flaws in Trump\xe2\x80\x99s billionaire strategy.

meanwhile\xe2\x80\xa6

John Heilemann and Joe Scarborough exchange inauguration notes.

PODCASTS

Dylan chats with the Times \xe2\x80\x99s Ben Mullin about the future of CNN on The Grill Room .

and\xe2\x80\xa6

Ourand and NBC Sports\xe2\x80\x99s Nicole Auerbach offer their CFP scorecards on The Varsity .

and\xe2\x80\xa6

and\xe2\x80\xa6

Matt and The Hollywood Reporter \xe2\x80\x99s Steve Zeitchik parse the Oscar nom narratives on The Town .

and\xe2\x80\xa6

Tara and Meghan McCain wade into the president\xe2\x80\x99s D.C. takeover on Somebody\xe2\x80\x99s Gotta Win .

and\xe2\x80\xa6

Lauren and the Times \xe2\x80\x99s Sapna Maheshwari predict how the TikTok ban (or not?) will play out on Fashion People .

and\xe2\x80\xa6

Peter and Eriq weigh in on a crucial CBS News lawsuit on The Powers That Be.

Billionaires at the Gate

On Monday morning, I was driving home from a short ski trip in Vermont when I tapped the YouTube TV app on my phone to listen in on CNN\xe2\x80\x99s coverage of the Trump II inaugural. The kids were in the backseat with their iPads and headphones\xe2\x80\x94a brand-safe environment, you might say, for my wife and me to overhear Jake Tapper and Dana Bash narrating the unfolding history. Cruising down an unspectacular stretch of I-87, just south of Albany, we exchanged glances as Bash noted yet another anomaly of this Boschian portrait of modern American life: a cohort of tech billionaires taking their seats next to Trump\xe2\x80\x99s family\xe2\x80\x94 in front of his incoming cabinet, the very people managing his administration. Somehow, it made perfect sense.

Silicon Valley\xe2\x80\x99s game of footsie with the president\xe2\x80\x94particularly the overtures of Sam Altman , Mark Zuckerberg , and Elon Musk \xe2\x80\x94have been endlessly chewed over yet somehow largely under-explored. Is it possible that they\xe2\x80\x99re all secret Trumpers with deeply held views on issues like immigration? Possibly, but I\xe2\x80\x99d surmise that they\xe2\x80\x99re also calculating realpolitik executives in charge of hundreds of thousands of employees and businesses whose market caps, combined, are in the multi-trillions of dollars. So it\xe2\x80\x99s also possible that they have strong views about the future of TikTok, the regulation of artificial intelligence, or the impact of policies, like tariffs, on their long-term business plans.

And, yes, it\xe2\x80\x99s also possible that their guts and manifold advisors have suggested that Trump will be at the peak of his powers for the first 18 months of his regime, before the reality of the midterms, the Constitution, and the self-interest of House members complexifies , to steal Bezos \xe2\x80\x99s coinage, the political hand he\xe2\x80\x99s been dealt. According to this theory, now would seem to be the best time for them to throw their weight around or simply smile before the camera and keep their inner thoughts, whatever they may be, to themselves while they transact. (The best negotiations, after all, are the ones that don\xe2\x80\x99t look like negotiations\xe2\x80\xa6) Indeed, the popular assessment of this dynamic posits that Trump is completely using these guys, but what if the relationship is reciprocal and they\xe2\x80\x99re all just playing a much longer game?


Anyway, I won\xe2\x80\x99t pretend to be able to read anyone\xe2\x80\x99s mind, but even Trump\xe2\x80\x99s short-term political calculus seems a bit suspicious. As Peter Hamby notes in his excellent piece Trump\xe2\x80\x99s Billionaire Arbitrage , the optics of the inaugural weren\xe2\x80\x99t just uncomfortable for his billionaire pals\xe2\x80\x94they were also a misinterpretation of the so-called mandate that his sweeping election afforded him. \xe2\x80\x9cCan Trump plausibly claim to be standing up to powerful tech overlords and moneyed interests when every photo and camera angle shows him surrounded by America\xe2\x80\x99s private sector royalty?\xe2\x80\x9d Peter perspicaciously noted. \xe2\x80\x9cThought about another way: Trump has always derived his power from running against enemies\xe2\x80\x94hated elites and the establishment. But who do you run against when you win so bigly that your enemies just give up and join your team?\xe2\x80\x9d

The following day, I\xe2\x80\x99d find out that my patronage of CNN was, itself, an outlier. As our partner Dylan Byers explained in his latest masterstroke, The Contracting News Network , CNN\xe2\x80\x99s coverage of the inaugural averaged less than one-fifth of the audience it had driven during Biden \xe2\x80\x99s coronation a mere four years earlier. Between 11:30 a.m. and 1 p.m. ET on Monday, only 1.7 million people were watching CNN, down from 10 million in 2021.


In many ways, CNN would steal the news cycle from Trump shortly after the inauguration, as C.E.O. Mark Thompson confessed to the Times what Dylan had reported in Puck three months ago\xe2\x80\x94that the company will shed hundreds of jobs while it invests in hiring more than 100 new employees to architect its digital future. It\xe2\x80\x99s an uncertain plan, but no one has a better idea. Dylan captures the nuances of this strategy, and there are many of them, in his excellent follow-up, CNN After CNN .

Indeed, the news has never been more complicated, and the way we receive it has never been more balkanized, and yet both matter more than ever. These are the stories of our time, and precisely what you should expect to read in Puck.

Have a great weekend,

Jon
